sections.subtitle: Match Overview and League Standings
sections.text: Getafe, sitting at 13th in the league with 39 points, hosts Real Madrid, who are in 2nd place with an impressive 75 points, in Round 33 of the La Liga season at the Coliseum Alfonso Pérez in Getafe. This matchup promises a stark contrast in form and ambition, as Real Madrid aim to close the gap at the top, while Getafe look to secure a mid-table finish. The disparity in league positions highlights the challenge for the home side, who face one of the strongest teams in Spain. With Real Madrid’s attacking prowess and Getafe’s defensive resilience, this encounter could hinge on fine margins.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head History
sections.text: In their last 5 matchups, Real Madrid have dominated Getafe, winning all 5 encounters, including the most recent clash on December 1, 2024, which ended 2-0 in favor of Real Madrid. During that game, Antonio Rüdiger was named Player of the Match with a Sofascore rating of 7.9. Historically, Real Madrid have won 6 consecutive head-to-head games, often being the first to score in 5 of the last 6 matches. Additionally, Getafe have failed to keep a clean sheet in their last 6 meetings with Real Madrid, while 8 of their last 9 encounters have seen less than 2.5 goals, suggesting a pattern of tight, low-scoring affairs.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - Getafe
sections.text: Getafe will rely on standout midfielder Luis Milla, who ranks 3rd in the league for key passes with 66, boasting an average Sofascore rating of 7.14 and contributing 4 assists this season. Defender Omar Alderete also stands out, leading the league with 13.8% of long balls in total passes and holding a strong rating of 7.06. Another player to watch is Mauro Arambarri, who has scored 9 goals against an expected goals (xG) value of 7.06, showing clinical finishing with an overperformance of 1.941. Up front, Borja Mayoral, with 4 goals and 1 assist, could be a threat despite a modest average rating of 6.74.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - Real Madrid
sections.text: Real Madrid’s attack will be spearheaded by Vinícius Júnior, who has 11 goals and 5 assists, ranking 2nd in the league for successful dribbles per game (2.9) and times fouled per game (2.4). Jude Bellingham, with 8 goals and ranking 3rd league-wide for assists with 8, will be crucial in midfield, despite underperforming his xG by -2.0312. Luka Modrić also brings creativity, ranking 4th in the league for big chances created with 15, alongside a rating of 7.3. Federico Valverde, in form with a recent Sofascore average of 7.66, adds dynamism with 6 goals and 4 assists.

sections.subtitle: Team Form and Statistics
sections.text: Getafe have conceded first in 6 of their last 8 matches, a worrying trend against a Real Madrid side that often strikes early, scoring 17 goals between the 31st and 45th minutes this season, representing 21.21% of their total goals. Getafe, however, score late, with 10 goals between the 76th and 90th minutes, accounting for 12.9% of their tally. Real Madrid’s games often feature over 2.5 goals in 6 of their last 8 matches, while Getafe’s matches tend to have under 10.5 corners in 8 of their last 9 games. Getafe rank 3rd league-wide for fewest goals conceded with 31, but their attack struggles, ranking 18th with only 31 goals scored.

sections.subtitle: Missing Players and Impact
sections.text: Getafe will miss midfielder Christantus Uche, who has 3 goals, 5 assists, and ranks 4th in the league for times fouled per game with 2.2, potentially impacting their midfield creativity. Real Madrid, meanwhile, are without Kylian Mbappé, a major blow with 24 goals (2nd in the league) and a Sofascore rating of 7.61 (3rd in the league), alongside 2.4 successful dribbles per game (4th league-wide). His absence could reduce Real Madrid’s cutting edge, though their depth remains formidable. Both teams will need to adapt, with Getafe also missing defenders like Diego Rico and Real Madrid lacking Éder Militão.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights and Discipline
sections.text: Getafe are known for their physicality, committing the 2nd most fouls in the league with 550 and averaging 2.79 yellow cards per game, which could lead to disciplinary issues against Real Madrid’s flair players like Vinícius Júnior. Real Madrid, on the other hand, excel in late-game scenarios, with 30.3% of their goals coming between the 76th and 90th minutes. Getafe’s high ranking in tackles per game (17.09, 2nd in the league) and clearances per game (26.91, 4th in the league) suggests a defensive setup, but their low possession (42.06%, 18th in the league) could invite pressure.

sections.subtitle: Referee and Match Dynamics
sections.text: The match will be officiated by José María Sánchez Martínez, who averages 5.6 yellow cards and 0.25 red cards per game, indicating a likelihood of bookings given Getafe’s foul-heavy approach and Real Madrid’s dribbling threats. With head-to-head trends showing less than 10.5 corners in 4 of the last 5 meetings, and Getafe’s league rank of 16th for corners per game at 3.91, we can expect a game with limited set-piece action. The referee’s tendency for cards might play a role in a potentially heated encounter at the Coliseum Alfonso Pérez.
